sections.paragraph:
χείρ, χειρός, ἡ [etym. complex]

—1. ‘the body part with fingers’, hand

—a. w. focus on anatomical element Mt 3:12 al.

—b. w. focus on functioning aspect, its use in writing to emphasize authenticity or strong personal interest 1 Cor 16:21; Gal 6:11; Col 4:18; 2 Th 3:17; Phlm 19.

—2. in imagery of the hand as agent for powerful activity hand

—a. of God Lk 1:66; J 10:29; 13:3; Ac 7:50; 13:11; Hb 1:10; 2:7 v.l; 10:31; of Christ J 3:35; 10:28; of an angel Ac 7:35.

—b. of a hostile entity Mt 17:22; 26:45; Lk 24:7; J 10:39; Ac 12:11; 21:11b; 28:17; 2 Cor 11:33.
